title: Federal Open Market Committee announces approval of updates to its Statement on Longer-Run Goals and Monetary Policy Strategy
summary: The FOMC unanimously approved updates to its Statement on Longer-Run Goals and Monetary Policy Strategy on August 22, 2025, marking the third review of the framework since its adoption in 2012. The updates reflect lessons from the past five years and aim to enhance transparency, accountability, and effectiveness of monetary policy. Notably, the Committee's 2% longer-run inflation goal was not a focus of this review.
key_points: The FOMC unanimously approved updates to its Statement on Longer-Run Goals and Monetary Policy Strategy
The original framework statement was first adopted in 2012, with the first public review in 2019-20
The 2025 review was announced in November 2024 and included Fed Listens events at Reserve Banks and a research conference in May 2025
The 2% longer-run inflation goal was explicitly not a focus of the 2025 review
The updated statement emphasizes promoting maximum employment and stable prices for the benefit of all Americans
why_it_matters: This framework review shapes how the Fed communicates and conducts monetary policy over the medium to long term. While no change to the 2% inflation target was made, the updated language could signal shifts in how the Committee balances its dual mandate, potentially affecting market expectations for future policy conduct.
macro_context: The framework update provides foundational guidance for FOMC policy decisions. Investors monitor these statements for signals about how the Committee weights employment versus inflation, and how it might respond to various economic scenarios. The fact that the 2% target was not changed suggests continuity in the inflation anchor.

issues_found: Source is thin on specifics regarding actual content of the updated framework statement
No direct quotes from the updated statement included
Full text of changes only available via linked PDF (not included in source body)
Published approximately 7 months ago (historical record beyond 90-day threshold)

human_review.notes: The source is a high-level announcement that framework updates were approved but does not detail what those updates actually are. Human review should verify whether the full updated statement PDF should be retrieved and analyzed separately for a complete record.

notes: Source is a press release announcing but not detailing the framework updates. The actual revised Statement on Longer-Run Goals and Monetary Policy Strategy is referenced as a PDF link. For a complete picture of what changed in the framework, the linked PDF would need to be retrieved and compared to the prior version.
